claims.where: src/bse/bse_jax.py:108-121; same math at bse_serial.py:62-64, bse_simple.py:101-131, bse_ring_comm.py:262-298
claims.detail: Exchange term is diagonal in k — the Σ_{k'} required by the BSE exchange kernel is missing. As written: VX[b,c,v,k] = (1/nk) Σ_{M,N} M*_X[k,c,v,M] V_q0[M,N] Σ_{c',v'} M_Y[k,c',v',N] X[b,c',v',k] (k retained through every einsum). The reference the code cites (Henneke 2020, vendored in src/bse/context/) Eq. (4-5) reads [V_A X](i_v i_c k) = (1/N_k) Σ_{μν} ū_{i_c k}(r̂_μ)u_{i_v k}(r̂_μ)Ṽ_{A,μν}·(Σ_{k'} Σ_{j_c} u_{j_c k'}(r̂_ν) Σ_{j_v} ū_{j_v k'}(r̂_ν)X(j_v j_c k')) — full k'-sum; correct einsums would be 'kcvN,bcvk->bN' then 'kcvM,bM->bcvk'. The transcription error is already in the design note (context/README.md:44 'S(ν,k) = Σ_{c',v'} M(k,c',v',ν) X(c',v',k)') and propagated to all 3 matvec implementations plus ring_matvec_correctness_check (bse_ring_comm.py:960-962), so internal cross-checks cannot catch it. Concrete wrong output: any system with non-negligible exchange (singlet-triplet splitting, 2D/molecular excitons) gets singlet energies computed with ~1/N_k of the exchange for coherent states; invisible in the Si validation because bulk-Si exchange is sub-meV against the documented ~3 meV ISDF agreement floor (STATUS.md) and spectra were compared at η=0.15 eV. W term is unaffected (its Σ_{k'} is the FFT convolution, matching Eq. 4-6).
claims.evidence: bse_jax.py:108 S_partial = jnp.einsum("kcvN,bcvk->bNk", M_Y, X); :114 U_partial = jnp.einsum("MN,bNk->bMk", V_q0, S); :118 VX_partial = jnp.einsum("kcvM,bMk->bcvk", jnp.conj(M_X), U) — k is a batch index in all three. Henneke md (context/) line 443-454: [V_A X](i_v i_c k) = Σ_{j_v,j_c,k'} V_A(...) X(j_v j_c k') ... ×(Σ_{k'}(Σ_{j_c} u_{j_c k'}(r̂_ν)(Σ_{j_v} ū_{j_v k'}(r̂_ν)X(j_v j_c k')))) \tag{4-5}

claims.detail: Zero callers via any mechanism (direct import, empty bse/__init__.py, -m invocations in sandbox runs/skills/scripts, string dispatch, tests). Also non-executable as written: @jax.jit without static_argnums makes nkx/nky/nkz tracers so apply_W line 146 reshape(..., nkx, nky, nkz) raises ConcretizationTypeError, and lax.psum(axis_name='x') is unbound outside shard_map; additionally the 'kcvN,bcvk->bNk' einsum needs band-replicated X while the canonical sh.X = P(None,'x','y',None) band-shards it, so it could never run on >1 device under the real shardings. Superseded by bse_ring_comm (ring/gather) and bse_simple (default 'simple').

claims.detail: Branch on jax.device_count()>1: the single-device path calls solve_bse without n_reorth/block_size/rtol/solver_kind/matvec_kind, so solve_bse's default n_reorth=10 (bse_lanczos.py:45) applies. The CLI's own --n-reorth help (lines 438-445) states partial-reorth windows 'give ghost eigenvalues that destroy per-state oscillator strengths' for spinor BSE. Concrete failure: the STATUS.md-recommended command with --n-reorth -1 (or --solver davidson) on 1 GPU silently runs single-vector Lanczos with n_reorth=10 → ghost eigenvalues, no warning. Violates the no-16-GPU-gating principle in spirit: the portable 1-GPU path is the degraded one.

claims.detail: Second return value is the ORIGINAL extent ('return jnp.pad(...), size'), yet callers bind it to n_val_pad/n_cond_pad and solve_bse_sharded sizes trial vectors as (bs, nc_pad, nv_pad, nk) from data['n_cond_pad']/['n_val_pad']. Whenever n_val (n_cond) is not a multiple of grid_y (grid_x), psi/eps are padded but the Lanczos vector keeps unpadded extents → einsum shape mismatch at the first matvec. Trigger: --n-val 5 --px 2 --py 2. All validated runs used multiples (pad==0), the only exercised case. Secondary hazard if shapes were fixed: bands are zero-padded (ψ=0, ε=0) so padded pairs form an exact ΔE=0 null space below the physical spectrum (ghost states for Davidson's diagonal preconditioner).

verdicts.reasoning: Re-derived Henneke Eq. (4-5) from src/bse/context/Henneke-2020-...md:443-454 by hand: S(ν) must sum over ALL k' (Σ_{k'} Σ_{j_c,j_v} ...), producing a single k-independent quantity before the μν contraction and re-broadcast to every output k. Checked all 4 live V-term implementations directly: bse_jax.py:108 'kcvN,bcvk->bNk', bse_serial.py:62 'kcvN,bcvk->bNk', bse_simple.py (same, with an explicit comment 'S[b,ν,k] = Σ_{c,v} M_Y[k,c,v,ν]·X[b,c,v,k]' at line ~101), and bse_ring_comm.py's production apply_V_ring (lines 228-298: A_local/S_total both carry a trailing nk_local axis, no cross-k accumulation, confirmed by reading the full step_y/step_x fori_loop bodies). All four keep k as a batch axis throughout encode->apply->decode, i.e. compute a per-k diagonal projection, not the required full k'-sum. This is the actual matvec used in production (build_bse_ring_matvec calls apply_V_ring_only, and absorption_haydock.py / bse_feast.py / bse_lanczos.py all route through build_bse_ring_matvec), so the bug is not confined to a dead code path -- it affects the validated Si comparison in STATUS.md too. The design doc's own pseudocode (context/README.md:44 'S(ν,k) = Σ_{c',v'} M(k,c',v',ν) X(c',v',k)') encodes the same missing-k'-sum error, explaining why 3 independent from-scratch implementations and the correctness self-checks all agree with each other while being wrong relative to the cited reference. The magnitude claim ('~1/N_k of exchange') is a plausible but unverified rationalization for why Si passed at 3 meV; I did not independently verify that number, but the core defect (missing Σ_k') is unambiguous from direct einsum/index inspection against the paper's stated formula.

Enumerated call mechanisms: grep -rn 'apply_bse_hamiltonian\b' / '\bapply_V\b' / '\bapply_W\b' across sources/lorrax_D (src, docs) turned up only __all__ entries, the bse_jax.py:67-160 definitions themselves (calling each other), and prose mentions in context/*.md (design notes, not code). grep of test_bse.py and test_davidson_bse.py for these three names: zero matches (test_bse.py imports apply_D and compute_pair_amplitude from .bse_jax, not the three claimed-dead names). grep of docs/, and (no tools/ or scripts/ dir exists under sources/lorrax_D -- only docs/) confirm no CLI/argparse/-m invocation reaches them; __main__'s only path that touches bse_jax-module physics is --debug-parallelism -> _main_random_demo(), which explicitly calls apply_bse_hamiltonian_single_device (the bse_serial.py copy), never the bse_jax.py-local apply_bse_hamiltonian. bse_ring_comm.py:25 imports apply_D/apply_bse_hamiltonian_single_device/compute_pair_amplitude from .bse_serial, confirming the claimed-dead trio is bypassed even by its own sibling module. Non-executability corroborated independently: apply_bse_hamiltonian is @jax.jit with nkx/nky/nkz as plain int args (no static_argnums) -> tracer ints hit .reshape(...,nkx,nky,nkz) in apply_W (ConcretizationTypeError), and apply_V/apply_W use lax.psum(axis_name='x'/'y') with no enclosing shard_map/pmap when called via the bare @jax.jit wrapper (unbound axis name). No mechanism revives this code.

grep -n 'ring.timing' src (hyphen or underscore) returns only the argparse.add_argument line (bse_jax.py:481) and its .pyc cache; args.ring_timing is read nowhere. Went further than the claim: build_bse_ring_matvec (bse_ring_comm.py:340-345) does have a live per-term `timed=` kwarg that wires up common.timing sections (lines 452-465), but grep -n 'timed=True|build_bse_ring_matvec(' across src/bse shows every call site (bse_feast.py, bse_lanczos.py, bse_pseudopoles.py, absorption_haydock.py, bse_kpm.py, bse_w_exact.py, feast_*sweep.py) omits `timed=`, so it defaults False everywhere -- the whole timed-matvec branch is itself unreachable from any current caller, and --ring-timing was never wired to it. The claim's git-provenance citation (906dd31, a82d91b) does not actually check out: neither commit's diff contains the string 'ring_timing' (checked git show --stat and grep on both, against src/isdf/bse_isdf/bse_jax.py, the file's pre-rename path) -- that part of the evidence is unsubstantiated/likely a citation error by the reader, but it doesn't change the underlying verdict: the flag is parsed, unread, and no live code path depends on it.

Confirmed directly: bse_jax.py:325-328 single-device branch calls solve_bse(psi_c, psi_v, eps_c, eps_v, W_q, V_q0, nkx, nky, nkz, n_eig=..., max_iter=..., include_W=...) with no n_reorth kwarg, so bse_lanczos.py:45's default n_reorth=10 silently applies regardless of the CLI value in args.n_reorth (whose own default is -1 = 'full reorth', per the help text at bse_jax.py:439-444 which explicitly warns partial windows give 'ghost eigenvalues that destroy per-state oscillator strengths' for spinor BSE). STATUS.md's own 'Recommended usage' block passes --n-reorth -1 explicitly, and neither STATUS.md nor BGW_COMPARE.md documents or warns about this serial-path silent override -- grepped both for 'single-device'/'n_devices'/'1 GPU'/'serial path', zero hits. So this is not a documented BGW-compat convention; it's an unflagged silent degradation whenever jax.device_count()==1 (portable/CPU/1-GPU path -- exactly the case the sandbox's no-16-GPU-gating rule cares about).

Grepped add_argument lines in bse_feast.py and bse_kpm.py for eqp/n-occ/n_occ: zero matches in either file. Grepped the full body of both files for the strings 'eqp' and 'n_occ': zero matches anywhere, confirming neither module has any code path that could apply QP corrections or resolve an explicit occupied-band count. Re-read bse_jax.py's argv-rebuild blocks for both dispatch branches (kpm_argv at lines 516-527, feast argv at lines 543-602): neither list includes '--eqp' or '--n-occ' despite args.eqp/args.n_occ existing on the top-level parser (lines 477-478). Since args.bse defaults False, the *default* invocation of `python -m bse.bse_jax -i ... --eqp ...` (no --lanczos) takes the FEAST branch and silently drops the correction. BGW_COMPARE.md:13-14/96 independently documents that missing eqp corrections cause a 50-200 meV eigenvalue offset, and its only worked examples for bse.bse_jax explicitly add --lanczos --tda -- the FEAST/KPM paths are simply never exercised with QP corrections in the documented workflow, so this is a real, unguarded footgun rather than a known/intentional convention. parse_known_args at line 484 (silently swallowing unrecognized flags) compounds the risk, also confirmed as written.

verdicts.symbol: unclamped user band counts at write/EQP
verdicts.reasoning: (a) write path: write_eigenvectors_stream is called at bse_jax.py:335-345 with the raw n_val/n_cond function parameters of _preview_lanczos (never reassigned from clamped values). bse_io.py:83 allocates the HDF5 dataset with shape (1, n_write, nk, n_cond, n_val, ns, 2) from those same unclamped args, while the clamping (bse_io.py:426-427 in the serial _load_ring_subset path, and :887-888 in the sharded loader) happens to LOCAL variables inside the loader functions and is never propagated back to _preview_lanczos's n_val/n_cond. Confirmed the actual write at bse_io.py:100-101 (`evec_dset[0,i,:,:,:,:,0] = vec.real`) assigns the clamped/actual-shaped `vec` into a dataset slice sized by the unclamped n_val/n_cond -- a genuine shape mismatch (h5py raises on mismatched fancy-index assignment) whenever the CLI's n_val/n_cond exceed availability and --write-eigs is used. (b) EQP branch: bse_jax.py:254-255 builds cond_idx = np.arange(n_occ_eff, n_occ_eff + n_cond) from the same unclamped CLI n_cond, then bse_jax.py:256-257 indexes enk_full_np[:, val_idx]/[:, cond_idx] directly with no clamp -- out-of-range indices there raise IndexError/produce garbage. Both failure modes are reachable with an ordinary --n-val/--n-cond CLI overshoot and are not addressed anywhere else in the file.

Confirmed by direct read: bse_io.py:139-146 -- `size = x.shape[axis]` is captured BEFORE padding (line 140), and both the pad and no-pad branches return that pre-pad `size` (line 143's early return, and line 146's `return jnp.pad(...), size`). At bse_io.py:450-451, `psi_v_X, n_val_pad = _pad_axis_to_multiple(psi_v_X, axis=1, multiple=grid_y)` binds the pre-pad extent to a variable literally named n_val_pad, then bse_io.py:533-534 stuffs that same wrong value into data['n_val_pad']/data['n_cond_pad'] (only the grid_x==1 and grid_y==1 fallback at lines 454-456 computes the correct post-pad shape.array-based value). bse_lanczos.py:140-148 consumes data['n_cond_pad']/data['n_val_pad'] to size the Lanczos trial-vector shape (bs, nc_pad, nv_pad, nk) -- which will be smaller than the true padded psi_c_X/psi_v_X/eps_c/eps_v arrays whenever a pad actually occurred (n_val not a multiple of grid_y, or n_cond not a multiple of grid_x), producing an einsum shape mismatch at the first matvec. The trigger (--n-val 5 --px 2 --py 2, since grid_y=2 and 5%2!=0) is a plain CLI combination, not exotic. Checked STATUS.md/BGW_COMPARE.md's validated commands: all use n_val/n_cond ∈ {4,8} with px=py=2, all multiples of the grid -- consistent with the claim that this path is genuinely unexercised in the validated runs, so the bug is real but latent given current usage patterns.

verdicts.symbol: default kernel/solver + Ry/eV + BGW writer conventions
verdicts.reasoning: Sanity-checked each sub-point directly: (1) `use_rpa = args.rpa or not args.bse` with args.bse defaulting False confirms RPA-by-default; args.lanczos defaulting False confirms FEAST-by-default -- both bare code facts, matches STATUS.md's own module table calling this 'working' with recommended overrides. (2) grep confirms internal solve/matvec math stays in Ry (energy_diff_cv_k, apply_D, etc. have no eV conversion); RYD2EV=13.6056980659 is applied only at print/write boundaries (bse_jax.py:191/329-331, bse_io.py:41). (3) STATUS.md's 'Index ordering' section independently documents and justifies the valence-axis flip and eV write as deliberate BGW-compliance choices, citing BGW source line numbers (BSE/input_fi.f90:407) -- this is pre-existing, cross-referenced documentation, not a post-hoc excuse. (4) create_mesh_2d(devices=None) (bse_ring_comm.py:31-43) takes no px/py parameters and auto-factorizes jax.device_count(); _preview_lanczos calls it with no args (bse_jax.py:229), confirming --px/--py truly have no effect on the lanczos/FEAST-via-lanczos preview path (though ring_matvec_correctness_check at line 493-500 does consume args.px/args.py, so the flag isn't globally dead, just ignored on this one path). (5) grep for nspin==1/singlet-factor branches across all of src/bse: zero hits, confirming no scalar-relativistic (non-spinor) code path exists, consistent with context/README.md:20's explicit statement that D+V-W (not D+2V-W) is the deliberate spinor convention. All five sub-claims check out as documented/intentional rather than unflagged bugs.

All four sub-items verified directly in the file: bse_jax.py:27 `from .bse_io import _find_restart_file, _load_ring_subset` and :39 `from .bse_io import write_eigenvectors_stream` are indeed two separate import statements from the same module (should be one line). Line 248 `with __import__('h5py').File(restart_file, "r") as f:` and line 244's `import numpy as _np` (inline, inside the eqp branch of _preview_lanczos) both confirmed present as written. Lines 456-459 confirm the `--gather-t` action='store_true' argparse entry with help text '(Deprecated alias for --matvec-kind=gather)', consumed at line 622 (`matvec_kind=("gather" if args.gather_t else args.matvec_kind)`) -- functional but flagged by its own docstring as a deprecated duplicate surface. docs/architecture/codebase.md:115 and :467 both still reference `bse_isdf.py` / `bse.bse_isdf:main`; confirmed `ls src/bse` has no bse_isdf.py, and pyproject.toml:36 has an explicit comment stating the `lorrax-bse` entry point 'was removed' and its target 'bse.bse_isdf:main' -- so the docs page is stale relative to a change the source tree already acknowledges. None of these are load-bearing; all are accurately classified as low-severity residue.

claims.detail: main() crashes with KeyError 'W_R' at the first GMRES solve in EVERY mode (including --rpa). The loader's return dict (bse_io.py:515-536) contains no 'W_R'; main never adds it; gmres_solve_sharded_jit → _apply_shifted_matvec unconditionally indexes data['W_R'] at trace time, before include_W can matter. Every other consumer sets it post-load (bse_feast.py:505-507, bse_pseudopoles.py:252-254, feast_zolo_sweep.py:123-124, feast_ellipse_mixed_sweep.py:98-99, bse_kpm.py:153-155). git log -S 'W_R' -- src/bse/bse_w_exact.py is empty — never set in available history; file was deleted (a0da0a5) and restored (fe5e3e8 'restore ... W-exact') without a validation run. One-line fix: data['W_R'] = jnp.fft.ifftn(data['W_q'], axes=(2,3,4), norm='ortho'). Verdict: CONFIRMED (all data['W_R'] setters enumerated repo-wide; none reachable from bse_w_exact).
claims.evidence: bse_w_exact.py:139-147 `x, _ = gmres_solve_sharded_jit(matvec, diag_h, z, rhs, data, ...)`; bse_feast.py:55-71 `_apply_shifted_matvec(... data["W_R"], data["V_q0"])`; bse_feast.py:139 `r0 = b - _apply_shifted_matvec(matvec, x0, z, data)`; bse_io.py:515-536 return dict keys = psi_c_X/psi_c_Y/psi_v_X/psi_v_Y/eps_c/eps_v/W_q/V_q0/g0_X/g0_Y/nkx/nky/nkz/n_rmu/n_rmu_pad/n_val/n_cond/n_val_pad/n_cond_pad/fermi_energy — no W_R; grep '"W_R"' src/bse → setters only in bse_feast/bse_pseudopoles/feast sweeps/bse_kpm

claims.detail: The exchange (V) term is applied k-block-diagonally: einsum 'kcvN,bcvk->bNk' keeps k as a batch axis (k in both operands AND output ⇒ never summed), and the back-contraction 'kcvM,bMk->bcvk' reuses the same k. Net operator V_code[cvk,c'v'k'] = δ_{kk'}(1/nk)Σ_{μν}conj(M[k,cv,μ])V_q0[μν]M[k,c'v',ν]. The module's own reference algorithm (Henneke 2020 context copy, Eq. 2-17/4-3: V_A(ivick, jvjck') dense in (k,k'); Eq. 4-5 matvec has explicit Σ_{k'}) and its design doc (context/gpt5.2suggestion.md §3.1: 'p(b,ν) = Σ_k P(b,k,ν)... sum over k locally') both require the Σ_k'; BGW's exchange kernel is likewise dense in (k,k'). Failure scenario: any observable sensitive to inter-k exchange coupling — singlet/triplet & dark/bright exciton structure, and the RPA resummation inside bse_w_exact's (z−D−V)^{-1} where each bubble insertion collapses to its k-diagonal — is structurally wrong; for k-coherent low excitons the expectation error is only second-order in the k-variation of M_k·a, consistent with the reported ~3 meV Si eigenvalue and 1.5% Haydock-peak BGW agreement (both exchange-insensitive at that scale), so existing validations cannot detect it. All implementations share the pattern and are only cross-checked against each other. Conjugation sides (contract M=conj(ψc)ψv with X, back-project conj(M)) are the Rohlfing-Louie convention (elementwise conjugate of Henneke 4-5) — that part is convention, not bug. Verdict: PLAUSIBLE (index math certain; 'deliberate approximation' not fully excluded, though no code comment or doc claims it).
claims.evidence: bse_serial.py:62 `S_V = jnp.einsum("kcvN,bcvk->bNk", M, X) / sqrt_nk`; :63 `U_V = jnp.einsum("MN,bNk->bMk", V_q0, S_V)`; :64 `V_term = jnp.einsum("kcvM,bMk->bcvk", jnp.conj(M), U_V) / sqrt_nk` — per-element V_term[b,c,v,k]=(1/nk)Σ_{μν}conj(M[k,c,v,μ])V_q0[μν]Σ_{c'v'}M[k,c',v',ν]X[b,c',v',k], no Σ_k'. Henneke context doc line ~425 Eq (4-5): '(1/N_k)Σ_μ ū_{ick}(r̂_μ)u_{ivk}(r̂_μ){Σ_ν Ṽ_{A,μν}(Σ_{k'}(Σ_{jc}u_{jck'}(r̂_ν)(Σ_{jv}ū_{jvk'}(r̂_ν)X(jvjck'))))}'; gpt5.2suggestion.md §3.1 'p(b_{Y_b},ν_{Y_ν}) = Σ_k P(b,k,ν)  Now sum over k locally'

claims.detail: n_rmu = int(data['V_q0'].shape[0]) is the PADDED extent n_rmu_pad (loader pads μ/ν to the mesh-product multiple with zero rows/cols, trim=False), while the logical count data['n_rmu'] is ignored. When n_rmu % (px·py) != 0, --n-cols random sampling and the 'out of N_mu' print include padding columns; V_q0[:,ν0]=0 there ⇒ f=0 ⇒ x=0 ⇒ identically zero Wc columns — silently wasted solves and skewed column statistics. verdicts.reasoning: Reproduced index math directly: bse_io.py return dict (lines 515-536) has no 'W_R' key. bse_feast.py:55-71 _apply_shifted_matvec does plain Python dict access data["W_R"] (not a jax-traced lookup, so it fails immediately, not merely at trace time under some condition), called from _get_gmres_solver's _solve closure (line 139), called by gmres_solve_sharded_jit, called by bse_w_exact.main at line 139 for EVERY mode since include_W only changes which matvec factory is used, not whether _apply_shifted_matvec is called. Grepped every 'data["W_R"] =' setter repo-wide: bse_feast.py:505/507 (main FEAST path), 516/518 (fp32 rebuild), 714/716; bse_pseudopoles.py:252/254; feast_zolo_sweep.py:123-124; feast_ellipse_mixed_sweep.py:98-99; bse_kpm.py:153/155; davidson_absorption.py builds W_R locally and passes it as a positional arg, not via data dict. None of these setters are reachable from bse_w_exact.py, which never touches 'W_R'. git log -S'W_R' -- src/bse/bse_w_exact.py across all branches returns empty, confirming it was never set even before the delete/restore (a0da0a5 -> fe5e3e8). No dispatch mechanism (no console_scripts entry, no runs/skills/tools reference invoking bse_w_exact.main) changes this -- it is a straightforward reachable-and-broken CLI entry point.

Re-derived the einsum index algebra myself in bse_serial.py:62-64: S_V[b,N,k] = sum_{c,v} M[k,c,v,N]*X[b,c,v,k] (k retained as batch axis, not summed -- this is standard einsum semantics: an index present in both operands AND the output is never contracted); U_V[b,M,k] likewise stays diagonal in k; V_term[b,c,v,k] = sum_M conj(M[k,c,v,M])*U_V[b,M,k], still diagonal in k. Cross-checked apply_V_ring in bse_ring_comm.py:228-298: S_total explicitly carries a trailing (...,k) axis through every ring-reduction step (jnp.sum(buf,axis=1) sums only over c, never over k), and the final VX_partial einsum again keeps k on both sides. Same k-diagonal pattern appears in the reference V_ref at bse_ring_comm.py:960-962. This contradicts the repo's own design docs: Henneke-2020 context doc explicit Eq.(4-5) has an outer Σ_{k'} over ALL k', and gpt5.2suggestion.md §3.1 explicitly derives p(b,ν)=Σ_k P(b,k,ν) -- a k-independent aggregate formed by summing over ALL k before applying V_q0 and re-expanding per-k in the final contraction. The shipped code never performs this Σ_k/Σ_k' step in any of the four implementations. This matches standard BSE physics: the bare-Coulomb (q=0) exchange/local-field term is by construction dense/rank-structured across ALL (k,k') pairs (it's the term responsible for singlet-triplet splitting and LO-TO/depolarization effects) -- collapsing it to a k-diagonal operator silently drops the majority of the physical term. No comment or STATUS.md entry anywhere claims this collapse is a deliberate approximation; docstrings ('pair density at q=0 · V_q0 · pair density^H · X' in bse_simple.py) describe it in a way consistent with the correct (non-diagonal) term, not a deliberately truncated one. I upgrade the reader's own hedge from PLAUSIBLE to CONFIRMED given the unambiguous index math plus corroboration from both cited internal references.

Verified directly: bse_io.py:439-442 computes n_rmu_pad = padded_mu_extent(n_rmu, grid_x*grid_y) and reads V_q0/W_q at that padded extent with trim=False (lines 460-461); returned dict carries both 'n_rmu' (logical) and 'n_rmu_pad' (padded) as separate keys (lines 529-530). bse_w_exact.py:98 reads n_rmu = int(data['V_q0'].shape[0]), which is the padded extent, and uses it for --n-cols sampling range and the printed 'out of N_mu' count (lines 115-116), ignoring the logical data['n_rmu']. When the mesh product does not evenly divide n_rmu the padded columns are zero-filled, so solves on those indices are identically zero. Confirmed as described.

grep across src/tests/tools/scripts/docs for 'single_device_jit' returns only the definition (bse_serial.py:84) and bse_jax.py:30 import / :49 __all__ entry -- no call-with-parentheses anywhere. No dispatch/CLI/test reference found. Confirmed dead.

Verified bse_w_exact.py:120-136: dtype_r/dtype_c select fp32/fp64 but are only applied to `g` (line 127, an input-space unit vector), `rhs` (line 134/136). `data` dict (psi/V_q0/W_q, and the still-missing W_R) is never cast; no call to a _build_gmres_data_fp32-style helper exists in the file (grepped for it, zero hits), unlike bse_feast.py:514 (`data_gmres = _build_gmres_data_fp32(data)`) and bse_kpm.py:150-155 which explicitly rebuild W_R in fp32. Since the matvec closes over the original fp64 `data` dict, the dominant-cost operator still runs at complex128 regardless of the flag. Reclassifying from 'cruft' to effectively a correctness/false-advertising bug in the flag's behavior is reasonable, but I'll keep the reader's own softer framing since it doesn't crash or silently corrupt results, just fails to deliver the claimed speed/memory benefit -- 'cruft' as filed is defensible; evidence fully checks out either way.

Grepped the file body for 'math.', '@dataclass', 'NamedSharding(' and bare 'P(' usage outside the import line -- zero hits, confirming dataclass/math/NamedSharding/P are unused. Confirmed double bse_feast import at lines 13 and 22 (RY_TO_EV_DEFAULT+gmres_solve_sharded_jit vs build_preconditioner_diagonal_sharded, two separate from-imports of the same module). This is real but purely cosmetic dead-import cruft, not a functional bug -- keeping the reader's 'cruft' classification is correct; I flagged verdict as confirmed-bug only in the sense that the underlying evidence is accurate, not that it warrants bug-severity handling.

Confirmed 4+ independent hand-written copies: bse_serial.py (single-device reference), bse_simple.py (plain-jit, docstring at :11-15 literally argues its own ring alternative reasoning), bse_ring_comm.py (hand-rolled ppermute/all_gather ring + full versions), bse_jax.py apply_V/apply_W (hand-rolled psums) plus a locally redefined compute_pair_amplitude (bse_jax.py:94-95) duplicating bse_serial.py:27-29, even though bse_jax.py separately imports apply_bse_hamiltonian_single_device[_jit]/symmetrize_W_q FROM bse_serial (lines 28-32) -- i.e. it imports some symbols from bse_serial but silently reimplements another rather than importing it, exactly the redundancy pattern flagged. verdicts.symbol: H = D + V - W (no singlet factor 2) and Ry units
verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed bse_serial.py:80 returns D_term + V_term - W_term with no factor of 2, and psi tensors carry an explicit spinor axis 's' (nspinor read from dataset shape[2] in bse_io.py:190, contracted via 'kcsm,kvsm->kcvm' pair-amplitude einsums that already sum over s). Henneke's spin-restricted singlet formula's factor-2 exists specifically to account for the two spin channels that the bispinor formalism instead sums explicitly via the s-axis contraction, so factor 1 is the physically correct coefficient for the FR-bispinor (nspinor=2) production case -- this is a genuine convention difference, not a missing factor. Verified Ry-to-eV boundary conversion is confined to CLI args (bse_w_exact.py:118 z=(omega_ev+i*eta_ev)/ry_to_ev) and STATUS.md:25-34 documents the valence-axis-flip convention lives in bse_io.write_eigenvectors_stream, not in these files. Correctly filed as convention, not bug.

This is the same rot class as the W_R bug and is accurately characterized as dead/broken code, matching the reader's framing that ties it to the same restore-without-validation pattern.

claims.detail: The q=0 exchange V is k-DIAGONAL: per element (VX)[b,c,v,k] = (1/Nk) Sum_{mu,nu} conj(M[k,c,v,mu]) V_q0[mu,nu] Sum_{c'v'} Mbar[k,c',v',nu] X[b,c',v',k] — k is a batch index in every einsum ('kcvN,bcvk->bNk' keeps k on both sides), i.e. K^x_{cvk,c'v'k'} proportional to delta_kk'. Standard Q=0 BSE exchange couples ALL (k,k') (Rohlfing-Louie/BGW: (1/Nk) M*_cvk(G) v(G) M_c'v'k'(G) for every k,k' pair; BGW bsexmtxel computes all (ik,ikp) blocks), and the module's own spec doc src/bse/context/tda_and_pseudopoles.md section 2.2 defines rho[r_nu] = Sum_t R[t,r_nu] Z[t] over the FULL transition index t=(v,c,k). Code keeps only the k'=k block, so exchange vanishes as 1/Nk of the physical operator. Failure scenario: any singlet-vs-triplet observable or 2D system (MoS2/CrI3) where exchange is tens of meV — LORRAX would report near-triplet spectra while claiming singlet. The STATUS.md Si validations (3 meV eigenvalues, 1.5% peak eps2 vs BGW singlet absorption, eta=0.15 eV) cannot detect it because bulk-Si low-exciton exchange is meV-scale. The ancestor couple_k=True branch implemented exactly the missing Sum_k. Possibly a deliberate but undocumented approximation — needs a dedicated gate (BGW spin_triplet A/B or dense-kernel diff) before trusting singlet numbers.

claims.symbol: V-term conjugation, no singlet factor, Hermitian-B assumption
claims.detail: (1) V path computes bra=psi_c conj(psi_v), ket=conj(psi_c) psi_v — the transpose-conjugate of the textbook conj(Phi) V Phi form; equivalent for Hermitian V_q0 and acknowledged at bse_simple.py:95. (2) A = D + V - W carries NO singlet x2 on V: the spinor (SOC) convention; nspinor=1 spin-restricted singlet would need 2V and no caller injects it (all validated runs were spinor Si). (3) Full matvec computes Y_out = -B(X) - A(Y) assuming B Hermitian ('A and B are Hermitian in this formulation', l.666); with SOC/complex psi the coupling block is complex-symmetric, not Hermitian, in the standard formulation — untested because the B W-path crashes (claim 1). LORRAX-internal band order: v=0 = deepest valence; BGW flip lives only in bse_io per STATUS.md 'Index ordering'.

verdicts.reasoning: Reproduced the crash directly: np.einsum('kvsM,bvksN->bMNtsk', (2,3,2,4),(1,3,2,2,5)) raises 'output subscript t which never appeared in an input'; the valid A-side analog 'kctM,bcksN->bMNtsk' (line 121, and identically at 373/521/965) passes. Traced call graph: build_bse_ring_matvec_full's _matvec_impl unconditionally calls _apply_B(Y,...) for X_out and _apply_B(X,...) for Y_out (lines ~656-670); _apply_B only skips the encode_T_ring_B/encode_T_B_gather path when include_W is False. Grepped all call sites of build_bse_ring_matvec_full: bse_feast.py:494 (use_tda=False branch), bse_kpm.py:131, bse_w_exact.py:106, bse_pseudopoles.py:242 — none pass include_W=False in the non-TDA branch, so every one hits the broken einsum. Confirmed src/bse/test_bse.py has zero calls to build_bse_ring_matvec_full (only test_matvec/test_pair_amplitude/test_lanczos), and no other test file references it — matches reader's claim of zero coverage for full-BSE-with-W.

AST-parsed both function signatures at HEAD: args = ['mesh_xy','nkx','nky','nkz','timed','low_mem','include_W'], kwarg=None (no **kwargs). grep 'v_couples_k' src/bse/*.py shows bse_kpm.py:120-137 and bse_pseudopoles.py:231-248 both compute v_couples_k=bool(not include_W) and pass it as a kwarg to both builders — this raises TypeError: unexpected keyword argument at call time, verified by inspection of the exact positional/keyword arg list. Both bse_kpm.py:main (line 323) and bse_pseudopoles.py:main (line 550) are `if __name__=='__main__'` CLI entry points, so this is reachable via `python -m` / direct script invocation, not just an internal helper. Confirmed via git archaeology that the ancestor apply_V_ring (git show 81ca040) had `couple_k: bool=False` as a real kwonly param wired end-to-end from build_bse_ring_matvec(..., v_couples_k=False) down through apply_V_ring_only(..., couple_k=v_couples_k); the current apply_V_ring (HEAD) has zero such parameter at all — the wiring is not just missing at the builder level, it was deleted from the leaf function entirely. Reader's fe5e3e8 restoration citation checked out: git log shows bse_pseudopoles.py/bse_w_exact.py were deleted at a0da0a5 and restored at fe5e3e8, consistent with 'callers restored ... still use it'.

Re-derived the index math independently by tracing apply_V_ring line by line (lines 228-300): R_v/A accumulate sums over c (x-ring, `S=S+jnp.sum(buf,axis=1)`) and v (embedded in the y-ring step), producing S_total[b,N,k] — note k is retained as a live index, never summed. U_partial='MN,bNk->bMk' keeps k on both sides (line 289); VX_partial='kcvM,bMk->bcvk' again indexes U at the SAME k as M_X (line 297). This is unambiguously k-diagonal: V couples (c,v,k) only to (c',v',k), never to k'≠k. Checked apply_V_ring's current signature: it has NO couple_k/v_couples_k parameter at all (confirmed by AST dump — only X,psi_c_Y,psi_v_Y,psi_c_X,psi_v_X,V_q0,nk,px,py), so the k-diagonal behavior is hardcoded, unconditional, and applies to BOTH RPA and full-BSE modes today (worse than the reader's 'only affects full BSE' framing — it affects everything, since the coupling mechanism was deleted, not just mis-wired). Critically, this checkout's own design-spec doc (src/bse/context/tda_and_pseudopoles.md, sec 2.2, written by/for this exact module) defines the V-piece contraction as rho[r_nu] = Sum_t R[t,r_nu] Z[t] with t=(v,c,k) explicitly the FULL transition index (sec 0: 't ≡ (v,c,k)'), and states in the same section 'For static BSE exchange, the same topology applies' — i.e. the doc mandates summing over k for BOTH RPA and BSE, contradicting the k-diagonal implementation entirely. Also found a second, independent implementation in the same file (build_density_snapshot_operator, ~line 786) that DOES sum over k correctly ('Returns density-space vectors in r_mu (summed over k)'), proving the authors know the correct contraction elsewhere in the file but the main matvec path (apply_V_ring) doesn't use it. Not a BGW-compat convention — checked BerkeleyGW's bsemat construction conceptually (Rohlfing-Louie q=0 exchange loops over ALL (ik,ikp) pairs via the shared G-sum), which matches the doc's full-t formulation, not the k-diagonal code. This is a real, high-severity physics bug, and given claim 2 confirms the only escape hatch (couple_k) is unreachable, there is currently no way to get correct q=0 exchange out of this module.

claims.detail: T assembled with transposed beta blocks: with qr(Z_flat.T)=Q~R the Galerkin matrix needs T[(i+1)bs+a, i*bs+b] = <q~_a, z_b> = R[a,b], but code stores beta_j = R.T (line 96) and sets the sub-block to beta_j, i.e. R[b,a]; symmetrization keeps it. Per-block transposition is not spectrum-preserving: zero-alpha counterexample B=[[1,2],[0,3]], C=[[4,5],[0,6]] gives tr(T^4) cross-term 4*tr(B B^T C^T C)=4*869 (true) vs 4*tr(B^T B C C^T)=4*629 (code) -> wrong Ritz values for block_size>=2 with >=3 block iterations (and wrong eigvec coefficients already at 2 blocks). The jit twin stores beta=R directly (line 417) and is correct. Only reachable via solve_bse(use_block=True), which nothing at HEAD calls.

claims.detail: Final Krylov slot overwritten: Q allocated with exactly max_iter slots and q_{j+1} stored at min(j+1, M-1); at j=M-1 this clobbers q_{M-1} with q_M. Eigenvectors = (Q @ vecs_T[:, idx]).T (line 296 / 434-435 / 578-579) then multiply the T-eigenvector coefficient of q_{M-1} onto q_M — per-state error ~ sqrt(2)*|vecs_T[M-1,i]| in an off-basis direction (large exactly for unconverged Ritz pairs; the regime STATUS.md flags for per-state oscillator strengths). Eigenvalues (from T) unaffected. Non-jit twins are clean: simple_lanczos_eig allocates (n, max_iter+1) (line 172) and uses Q[:, :max_iter] (206); block_lanczos_eig appends to a list and takes Q_blocks[:n_blocks] (105,128) — evidence of oversight, not convention.

claims.detail: Zero-padded band slots are exact spurious eigenvectors below the exciton gap. Loader pads psi AND eps with zeros to mesh multiples (_pad_axis_to_multiple, jnp.pad mode='constant'). For padded conduction slot c': psi_c[k,c',:,:]=0 => M[k,c',v,mu]=0 and T gets no c' contribution => V and W terms vanish identically on that slot; D gives H X^ = (0 - eps_v[k,v]) X^ exactly. With VBM-referenced enk (fermi_energy=0.0 default) the (c', v=VBM) modes sit at ~0 Ry, BELOW every physical exciton; padded valence slots give +eps_c[k,c] inside the spectrum. Concrete: 4 GPUs (2x2 mesh), --n-cond 7 --n-val 8 -> nc_pad=8 and the lowest n_eig returned 'excitons' are nk*n_val spurious -eps_v modes. Davidson is worse: init_bse_subspace seeds lowest-dE transitions = the padded ones. Latent because validated runs used mesh-divisible band counts (8x8 on 2x2). No masking anywhere in solve_bse_sharded or the matvecs.

claims.detail: The Krylov storage Q (n_flat, max_iter) / Q_all (M, n_flat, bs) inside solve_bse_sharded's outer jit carries no sharding constraint (only the per-matvec reshape re-pins P(None,'x','y',None)) — XLA typically replicates it per device: 16*n_flat*max_iter bytes each, a memory wall at production sizes and a violation of the zero-replicated-intermediates principle. Separately, the +1e6 diagonal masking of inactive slots leaves the beta_{j_final-1} coupling to the first masked block in T, biasing checked and final Ritz values by O(|beta|^2/1e6) ~ 1e-6 Ry on early exit. Also no guard for n_eig > Krylov dim: argsort[:n_eig] silently truncates and the downstream reshape(n_eig, ...) raises.
claims.evidence: lanczos.py:251 'Q = jnp.zeros((n, max_iter), dtype=jnp.complex128)' (no sharding); bse_lanczos.py:253-255 constraint only inside matvec; lanczos.py:541-545 'LARGE = 1.0e6 ... claims.detail: Zero automated pytest coverage: nothing under tests/ (incl. multi_device, regression) imports bse_lanczos or solvers.lanczos. Only manual __main__ harnesses requiring a restart h5. All four bug claims above are testable with a 1-GPU synthetic Hermitian operator (per no-16-GPU-gating policy); the block-T transpose and final-slot overwrite need only a random matrix.
claims.evidence: grep -rn 'lanczos|solve_bse' tests/ -> empty; tests/multi_device, tests/regression -> empty; src/bse/test_bse.py:245 and test_davidson_bse.py are python -m modules, not pytest

verdicts.reasoning: Re-derived the recurrence: Z_flat (bs,n_flat) = R.T @ Q_next_flat with QR(Z_flat.T)=Q~R (code lines 95-96), so the code's stored beta_j=R.T is the TRANSPOSE of the standard block-Lanczos off-diagonal R. I numerically re-implemented block_lanczos_eig verbatim in numpy on a random 30x30 Hermitian H with block_size=2 and compared T's eigenvalues to the TRUE Galerkin projection Q_all^H H Q_all on the identical Krylov basis (the basis generation itself is unaffected by the beta-storage bug). Buggy-code eigenvalues: [-9.620,-8.224,-7.072,-5.683,-1.956,...]; true Galerkin eigenvalues: [-9.400,-8.164,-7.072,-5.901,-2.032,...] -- clearly different. Substituting beta_fixed = beta_blocks[i].T (i.e. R, undoing the transpose) into T reproduces the TRUE Galerkin eigenvalues exactly (bit-identical). This proves the transpose is a genuine sign/index bug for block_size>=2, and confirms the jit twin (block_lanczos_eig_jit, column-vector convention, beta_j=R directly at line 417-418) is correct and structurally different from the row-vector non-jit twin.

Confirmed the code: Q=(n,max_iter) allocated, fori_loop over j=0..max_iter-1, storing q_next at index min(j+1,max_iter-1) (line 281); at the final iteration j=max_iter-1 this recomputes an EXTRA vector q_max and overwrites slot max_iter-1 (which held the actual q_{max_iter-1} used to build alpha[max_iter-1] and needed for eigenvector reconstruction), while T only uses alpha[:] and beta[:-1] (line 289) so eigenvalues are unaffected. I reimplemented the exact algorithm in numpy (n=25, max_iter=8) and compared Rayleigh residuals ||Hv-lambda v|| for eigenvectors built from the buggy Q vs. a 'fixed' Q with the true last slot restored: buggy residuals were uniformly larger (e.g. state2: 4.46e0 buggy vs 2.28e0 fixed) and the excess residual correlated with |vecs_T[max_iter-1,i]| (the coefficient on the clobbered slot), exactly matching the claim's failure mode. block_lanczos_eig_jit (line 419-420) and block_lanczos_eig_jit_converged (line 516) do the identical min(j+1,M-1) overwrite pattern on Q_all, and for the converged variant this only fires when the while_loop runs to the max_iter bound without early convergence (the unconverged-state regime STATUS.md flags), matching the claim precisely.

Verified algebraically from bse_serial.py's apply_bse_hamiltonian_single_device: M=compute_pair_amplitude(psi_c,psi_v) contracts psi_c directly, so a zero-padded conduction slot c' (psi_c[k,c',:,:]=0, from _pad_axis_to_multiple's jnp.pad mode='constant', bse_io.py:139-146, applied by default pad_bands=True at bse_io.py:449-453/898-902) forces M[k,c',v,:]=0 for all v, which propagates through V_term (contracted against conj(M)) and W_term (contracted against conj(psi_c)) to give EXACTLY zero on that slot -- not approximately, algebraically, since every term touching c' carries a psi_c[...,c',...] or M[...,c',...] factor. That makes the padded direction an exact eigenvector of H with eigenvalue delta_E[k,c',v]=eps_c_pad[c']-eps_v[v] = 0-eps_v[v] (eps_v/eps_c padded with 0 too, bse_io.py:452-453, appended after the real bands so the real VBM survives at index n_val-1). I proved generally that for v=VBM this spurious eigenvalue (-eps_v[VBM]) is strictly BELOW the true minimum single-particle transition eps_c[CBM]-eps_v[VBM], by exactly eps_c[CBM]>0 -- i.e. it necessarily sits below every real transition energy, so a lowest-n_eig Lanczos/Davidson search will surface it. Confirmed init_bse_subspace (bse_davidson_helpers.py:61-91) explicitly seeds the lowest E_c-E_v transitions, so Davidson's initial subspace is contaminated too. No masking of padded slots exists anywhere in bse_lanczos.py or the matvecs (grepped).

Confirmed write_eigenvectors_stream (bse_io.py:81-103) allocates evec_dset with shape (1,n_write,nk,n_cond,n_val,ns,2) using the RAW CLI n_val/n_cond values passed straight through from bse_jax.py:335-345 (_preview_lanczos's own n_val/n_cond args, never the padded nc_pad/nv_pad). Meanwhile solve_bse_sharded returns eigenvectors reshaped to (n_eig,1,nc_pad,nv_pad,nk) (bse_lanczos.py:230,312). The per-state write at bse_io.py:102-103 assigns vec (shape (nk,nc_pad,nv_pad,1) after transpose/flip) into evec_dset[0,i,:,:,:,:,0] whose target slice shape is (nk,n_cond,n_val,ns) -- a shape mismatch precisely whenever padding occurred (n_cond % grid_x != 0 or n_val % grid_y != 0), i.e. the identical trigger condition as claim 3. This crashes only after the full (possibly expensive) solve completes.

Exhaustive grep for 'use_block' across the entire lorrax_D source tree (src, tests, scripts, tools, docs) returns only 3 hits: bse_lanczos.py:42 (default False param), bse_lanczos.py:82 (the gated branch itself), and src/bse/test_bse.py:257 (explicit use_block=False). Grepped runs/**/*.sh (via find + xargs, since a bare recursive grep over runs/ times out due to tree size) and skills/**/*.md for 'use_block'/'use-block' -- zero hits; the only BSE-invoking run script found (runs/Si/B_centroid_sweep_2026-04-27/run_bse_sweep.sh) calls bse.bse_jax with --bse --tda --lanczos and no block flag. bse_jax.py's CLI --block-size/--solver-kind wire into solve_bse_sharded's block_size (routed to block_lanczos_eig_jit/_converged, the JIT twins), never into solve_bse's use_block. block_lanczos_eig is re-exported (solvers/__init__.py:19, bse_jax.py:34/52) but has no other call site anywhere. Confirmed dead.

Confirmed matvec_block = partial(lambda X: apply_bse_hamiltonian_single_device(...)) (bse_lanczos.py:76-80) is a partial() around a lambda with zero bound args -- functionally identical to just using the lambda, a cosmetic no-op wrapper (only reachable via the dead use_block path so low priority). More substantively, _matvec_impl (bse_lanczos.py:54-60, @jax.jit-decorated but defined INSIDE solve_bse's function body) is re-created as a fresh closure object every call, so JAX's jit cache (keyed to function identity) cannot be reused across solve_bse invocations -- genuine recompilation-per-call. I verified the claim's cited evidence file exists and matches: runs/Si/04_si_4x4x4_bse/01_lorrax_bse_vcoul/profile_bse/compile_summary.json line ~638 literally reads 'never seen function: solve_bse.<locals>._matvec_impl id=... defined at .../bse_lanczos.py:47', confirming this is an observed, real compile-cost regression in production, not speculative. Since solve_bse is the live single-GPU fallback path (bse_jax.py:325), this is worth fixing, not merely benign.

verdicts.symbol: matvec_kind via data dict + stale STATUS default
verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed matvec_kind is threaded through data['matvec_kind'] (bse_lanczos.py:155, data.get default 'ring') rather than a keyword arg -- the only such smuggled parameter, a real API-shape convention issue but harmless. Confirmed doc drift: bse_jax.py argparse --matvec-kind default is literally 'ring' (line 447-449), matching the dict-fallback default ('ring', bse_lanczos.py:155), but STATUS.md:16 explicitly states 'fastest, default --matvec-kind=simple' -- this is stale documentation, not a runtime bug (the code is internally self-consistent; it just contradicts its own doc). No wrong physics results from this.

Confirmed the Davidson branch (bse_lanczos.py:188-231) never references rtol/check_every/n_reorth/block_size (all accepted by solve_bse_sharded's signature but silently unused in this branch). Confirmed atol reuse: davidson() called with tol=atol if atol>0 else 1e-8 (bse_lanczos.py:226) where davidson's 'tol' is a residual convergence threshold, vs. the converged-Lanczos path where atol is an eigenvalue-scale floor (lanczos.py:548, scale=max(|ev|,atol)) -- same parameter name, two different semantics. Confirmed n_iter_done is hardcoded to jnp.int32(max_iter) regardless of actual Davidson iterations (bse_lanczos.py:231) -- Davidson's own loop tracks 'it' internally but this is never propagated back. Confirmed return-type mismatch: solvers/davidson.py's davidson() docstring and code (line 268-271, 309-320) explicitly return eigenvalues as np.ndarray (via _to_host), while the Lanczos-path functions in solvers/lanczos.py return jnp arrays throughout. All accurately described; none of this produces wrong physics, so 'convention'/code-smell is the right bucket, not a computational bug.

Confirmed exactly: bse_jax.py:616 include_W = not (args.rpa or not args.bse); with args.bse default False (store_true), omitting --bse gives not args.bse=True, so include_W=False regardless of --rpa -- W is silently dropped. Confirmed STATUS.md's recommended usage block (line 102) explicitly includes --bse in every documented command. This is a real, well-documented footgun (a user forgetting --bse silently gets RPA excitons with no warning) but it is exactly the coded behavior working as designed with a permissive default, not a computational error -- correctly bucketed as a usage/convention gotcha rather than a bug.

Confirmed src/solvers/lanczos.py contains ZERO occurrences of 'sharding'/'NamedSharding'/'with_sharding_constraint'/'PartitionSpec' (grepped, no hits) -- Q/Q_all storage (lines 251,387,488) is a generic, mesh-agnostic array with no sharding annotation; only the matvec's own input reshape gets with_sharding_constraint applied inside bse_lanczos.py's caller-side matvec closures (line 199,255,284), not the Krylov buffer itself, so XLA's GSPMD is free to replicate it -- this matches the repo's own 'zero replicated intermediates' principle violation pattern. Re-derived the 1e6-mask perturbation claim: in block_lanczos_eig_jit_converged, when the loop runs to convergence at j_final<M-1, beta_all[j_final-1] (a REAL Lanczos coupling, computed during the last executed step) is still placed into T at the boundary between the last active block and the first +1e6-masked ghost block (_build_block_tridiag places beta_all[j] unconditionally for all j in 0..M-2, lines 313-320, and the masking only adds to the diagonal, lines 541-545/570-574) -- standard 2nd-order perturbation theory gives an eigenvalue shift ~|beta|^2/1e6, i.e. the claim's math is correct, though the magnitude is generically small. Also confirmed: no guard exists for n_eig > Krylov dim (T_size); argsort(...)[:n_eig] on a smaller array just returns fewer values without erroring, and the downstream .reshape(n_eig,...) at bse_lanczos.py:312 would then raise -- a real but edge-case robustness gap. All three sub-findings check out; correctly filed as architecture/robustness cleanup rather than a wrong-output-in-normal-operation bug.

Confirmed pyproject.toml: [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = ['tests'] with norecursedirs = ['archive'] -- pytest only ever collects tests/tests. Grepped tests/ (including tests/multi_device, tests/regression) for 'lanczos' or 'solve_bse': zero .py hits (only unrelated stale .h5 fixture files live under tests/archive/, which is excluded anyway). src/bse/test_bse.py and src/bse/test_davidson_bse.py, despite test_-prefixed names, live outside testpaths, use argparse (both files: 'parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(...)') and are guarded by 'if __name__ == "__main__":' (test_bse.py:381, test_davidson_bse.py:308) -- manual scripts requiring a restart .h5, not auto-discovered pytest tests. Confirmed genuinely zero automated coverage of the entire lanczos family, matching the claim exactly; and per the sandbox's no-16-GPU-gating policy, the bug claims above (transposed-beta, final-slot overwrite) are indeed 1-GPU-testable with a synthetic Hermitian matvec, as demonstrated by my own numpy reimplementations above.

claims.detail: The helper returns the PRE-pad size in both branches, but both loaders bind it to n_val_pad/n_cond_pad and export it as the padded trial-vector extent. Every downstream consumer (bse_lanczos.py:140-148 shape=(bs,nc_pad,nv_pad,nk); bse_feast.py:558; bse_kpm.py:79; bse_ring_comm.py:736-737 divisibility check) needs the padded value. Failure math: mesh 2x2, n_val=5 -> psi_v_X padded 5->6 (multiple=grid_y=2) but data['n_val_pad']=5; bse_ring_comm.py:737 raises 'must be divisible by px/py' (5%2!=0) even though psi was just padded to be divisible; simple-matvec path instead einsum-mismatches (X band dim 5 vs psi band dim 6). Net: non-mesh-divisible band counts can never run; masked because standard runs use nv=nc=4/8. Plausible root of the memory-flagged CrI3 small-nbnd band-sharding death mode.

claims.detail: Writes a file claiming BGW eigenvectors.h5 format but stores eigenvalues raw in Ry (docstring :39 'exciton energies in Ry'; BGW convention is eV per STATUS.md:30 and bse_io.py:36-40) and never flips the valence axis (BGW iv=1 = highest valence, STATUS.md:29; contrast bse_io.py:100 'vec = vec[:, :, ::-1]'). verdicts.reasoning: Read bse_io.py:139-146 directly: `size = x.shape[axis]` is captured BEFORE `jnp.pad`, and both the pad>0 branch and the pad==0 branch return `size` (pre-pad). Grepped every call site (`grep -n n_val_pad|n_cond_pad src/bse/*.py`): bse_io.py:450-456 binds the return to `n_val_pad`/`n_cond_pad` and exports them in the returned dict at :533-534; bse_lanczos.py:140-141, bse_feast.py:522-523/730-731, bse_kpm.py:73-74 all read `data['n_val_pad']`/`data['n_cond_pad']` and build trial vectors/masks of that exact shape; bse_ring_comm.py:736-737 has a hard divisibility assert on the same names. Confirmed no test or call site exercises a non-mesh-divisible band count (grepped `pad_bands` — no caller ever passes it, so `pad_bands=True` default always runs, and no test hits the mismatch branch). The claim's failure scenario (mesh 2x2, n_val=5, padded to 6 but n_val_pad reported as 5) reproduces exactly from the code; only masked when nv/nc already divide the mesh (pad==0, where size==shape trivially agree).

Read bse_io.py:233-234 (`n_rmu = dset.shape[6]`) and :250 (`dset[0,0,0,0,0,0,mu0:mu1,nu0:nu1]`) — hardwired to 8-D indexing, no layout shim (contrast the adjacent `_read_wq_sharded` at :280-324 which explicitly branches on ndim 8/6/3). Confirmed the current GW writer emits flat-q 3-D V_qmunu: gw_init.py:277 docstring says 'V_qmunu has shape (nq, μ, μ) (flat-q)'; gw_jax.py:264 'V_q = V_qmunu # flat-q (nq, μ, μ) — compute and restart alike'; file_io/tagged_arrays.py:91 writes `V_qmunu` straight through with that shape (`_write('V_qmunu', V_qmunu, mu_axes=(-2,-1))`, no reshape to 8-D). A 3-D dataset has no axis index 6 and `dset[0,0,0,0,0,0,...]` requests 6 leading scalar indices on a 3-axis array — both raise IndexError. Grepped `load_bse_data_from_restart_sharded` call sites (bse_jax.py:234, bse_feast.py:1169, bse_kpm.py:365, absorption_haydock.py:167, davidson_absorption.py:85, feast_sweep.py:574, feast_ellipse_mixed_sweep.py:275, feast_zolo_sweep.py:284, bse_w_exact.py:86, bse_pseudopoles.py:594, test_davidson_bse.py:67) — none pass a legacy 8-D restart; all read from current gw_jax restart files, so every multi-GPU BSE entry point is dead on current-format restarts.

Confirmed slab_io.py:190-196 `write_attr` docstring 'Write a small rank-0-only dataset' and the backend implementation (_slab_io_allgather.py:118-135) literally does `self._file.create_dataset(name, data=host)` — a top-level HDF5 DATASET, not `dset.attrs[name]=value`. file_io/tagged_arrays.py:81-82 calls `io.write_attr('kgrid', ...)`, so kgrid lands as `f['kgrid']`, never as an attribute on V_qmunu/W0_qmunu. The outer loader `load_bse_data_from_restart_sharded` correctly falls back to `elif 'kgrid' in f:` at bse_io.py:398-401 and resolves nkx/nky/nkz locally — but at :461 it calls `_read_wq_sharded(wq_dset, mu_per_x, nu_per_y, mesh_xy, n_rmu_pad, trim=False)` with no kgrid argument (function signature at :268-276 has no kgrid parameter). Inside, :309 checks only `dset.attrs['kgrid']` (i.e. the per-dataset HDF5 attribute, which per the write_attr proof above is never set) and raises ValueError at :312-317 for any flat-q file. Confirmed as a real follow-up bug once bug #2 is fixed, exactly as claimed.

Re-derived the indexing math: head_correction.py:773 does `V_qmunu.at[..., 0, 0, 0, :, :].add(...)`, which requires the array's last 5 axes to be (nkx,nky,nkz,mu,nu) — true for 8-D legacy, coincidentally true for 6-D (npol=1 so the extra two 0-indices are harmless), but for 3-D flat-q `(nq,mu,nu)` this indexes 5 concrete positions into a 3-axis array → IndexError ('too many indices'). Confirmed the call at bse_io.py:828 (`apply_q0_head_rank1(V_qmunu, W0_qmunu, ...)`) runs BEFORE the ndim shim at :846 (`if V_qmunu.ndim == 8:` ... else flat-q branch at :856-872) — i.e. on the raw, unnormalized on-disk array. Confirmed the current writer always produces the trigger condition: G0_mu_nu is written unconditionally alongside V_qmunu (gw_init.py:664, tagged_arrays.py:94), and vhead is populated from `head_static.vc0` in gw_output.py:231 in the standard restart-write path (not behind an opt-in flag), so `G0_mu_nu is not None and (vhead is not None or whead is not None)` at bse_io.py:817 is true for ordinary restarts. Single-device BSE (`_load_ring_subset`, called by e.g. bse_ring_comm.py, bse_jax.py's non-sharded branch) on any current-format restart with head data crashes at :828.

Read write_eigenvectors.py in full: docstring at :38 says 'exciton energies in Ry', dataset written raw at :141 with no RYD2EV multiplication anywhere in the file (grepped `jnp\.`, `13.6`, `eV` — none present besides the print statements that convert for display only, not for the stored dataset). No `::-1` valence flip anywhere in the file (grepped 'flip|::-1' — none). Cross-checked src/bse/STATUS.md:29-34, which documents BGW's opposite conventions (iv=1=highest valence; eV units) and explicitly says only `write_eigenvectors_stream` in bse_io.py performs the compliant conversion — confirming `write_eigenvectors_h5` is the divergent, non-compliant writer, not an intentional alternate convention. Reachability: grepped the whole repo (`grep -rn write_eigenvectors --include=*.py`) — only test_bse.py:39/361 (CLI test flag) and the file's own `__main__` CLI import it; no run script or skill invokes it (grepped runs/**/*.sh — zero hits; the only match on 'write_eigenvectors' in runs/ was BGW's own absorption.inp keyword, unrelated). So it is a real, reachable (CLI/test), producer of objectively wrong BGW-format output, not literally dead code.

Confirmed bse_io.py:81-85 sizes the `eigenvectors` dataset from the function's own `n_val`/`n_cond` parameters, and bse_jax.py:333-345 (`_preview_lanczos`) passes its own `n_val, n_cond` function arguments straight through — these are never reassigned to the loader's clamped/padded values (`load_bse_data_from_restart_sharded`'s internal `n_val = min(n_val, n_val_available)` at bse_io.py:426-427 is a local variable inside that function; it does not mutate the caller's `n_val` in bse_jax.py). Meanwhile `solve_bse_sharded` returns eigenvectors shaped `(n_eig, bs=1, nc_pad, nv_pad, nk)` per bse_lanczos.py:229 comment and :140-141 (`nc_pad`/`nv_pad` from `data['n_cond_pad']`/`data['n_val_pad']`). If the loader clamped (WFN has fewer bands than requested, printing the warning at bse_io.py:423-425) or padded (mesh-divisibility), `vec`'s actual shape differs from the dataset's declared shape and the h5py slice-assignment at :102 raises a shape-mismatch error. Confirmed as a real, reachable defect (not merely hypothetical) given the mismatch is possible on any restart with fewer available bands than requested, or on any padded multi-GPU mesh — both realistic BSE CLI usage patterns.

verdicts.symbol: hardcoded BGW metadata + silent W=V fallback + LORRAX-vs-BGW axis conventions
verdicts.reasoning: Verified all cited evidence is accurate: bse_io.py:44-47 hardcodes `ns=1; spin_kernel=3; ...`, :71 hardcodes `use_tda=1`; write_eigenvectors.py:79/130 does the same. Checked BerkeleyGW's own spec (documentation/file_formats/eigenvectors.h5.spec:45-48): spin_kernel=3 does mean 'spinor calculations' specifically, so a non-spinor run would carry misleading metadata for external BGW-format consumers — a real fidelity gap, but traced its only current call site: write_eigenvectors_stream is invoked solely from bse_jax.py:335, inside `_preview_lanczos`, which is only reachable from `main()` after the `if not use_tda: raise SystemExit(...)` guard at bse_jax.py:606-607 — i.e. in the current codebase this path is TDA-only already, so `use_tda=1` is not presently wrong at its one call site. Confirmed the silent W=V fallback at bse_io.py:376-379 (`wq_dset = vq_dset` with no print) is real but is a documented, deliberate 'unscreened as W-not-ready fallback' rather than an accidental swap. Confirmed STATUS.md:29-34 documents the valence-flip/Ry-eV conversions as the intended BGW-compat boundary. The reader's own classification (convention, explicitly noting these are 'conventions, not bugs') matches the evidence; no reclassification warranted, though the spin_kernel/use_tda hardcoding is a latent risk if a non-TDA or non-spinor write path is ever wired to this writer in the future.

Confirmed bse_io.py:644 `return int(w.nelec)` while the docstring at :615 claims 'Reads mf_header/kpoints/ifmax directly — authoritative'. Confirmed wfn_loader.py:161-162: `if np.size(self.ifmax) > 0: self.nelec = int(np.max(self.ifmax))` — i.e. `nelec` IS in fact derived from `ifmax` (the highest-occupied-band-index across k-points), just under a misleading name (it's an occupied-band COUNT, not a raw electron count, though for a fully-occupied-valence system with ifmax constant across k these coincide). Purely a naming/documentation hazard, not a functional defect — matches reader's own 'not a bug' framing.

verdicts.symbol: _load_ring_subset X init same-key real/imag; _find_restart_file oldest-file pick; fermi_energy==0.0 sentinel
verdicts.reasoning: Verified all three sub-claims exactly. (1) bse_io.py:914-917: `key = jax.random.PRNGKey(0)` is reused for both the real and imaginary `jax.random.normal(key, ...)` calls with identical shape — JAX's normal() is a deterministic function of (key, shape), so `imag(X) == real(X)` elementwise for every element; this collapses the trial vector to a constant e^{iπ/4} phase, losing one real degree of freedom in the Lanczos/FEAST start vector (harmless for eventual convergence, since it's just a start vector, but as claimed a real loss of randomness). (2) `_find_restart_file` at :756-764: `sorted(glob.glob(...))` sorts lexicographically ascending, and the loop returns the FIRST existing path — for ISO-like timestamped filenames, ascending lexicographic order is ascending chronological order, so this returns the OLDEST matching file, a genuine stale-file hazard in a reused directory. (3) bse_io.py:417 (and the sharded loader's :416-417 pass-through): confirmed `fermi_energy=fermi_energy if fermi_energy != 0.0 else None` treats an explicit 0.0 Ry hint as 'unset', making an exact-zero Fermi-energy hint unpassable — confirmed as stated, a real (if narrow) edge-case gap rather than a bug affecting the common case (0.0 as a genuine physical Fermi level referenced to itself is an unlikely input, since resolve_n_occ's own docstring says fermi_energy is a fallback hint used only when ifmax/n_occ aren't otherwise resolvable).

Concrete wrong output: 'python -m bse.bse_jax --feast-window1 3 4' solves KPM-derived windows, not [3,4] eV.

claims.symbol: run_feast_ritz random start vectors vs zero-padded bands
claims.detail: bse_io zero-pads psi AND eps to shard multiples (mode='constant'). A padded-valence channel has psi_v=0 so V=W=0 and is an EXACT eigenchannel with eigenvalue eps_c(k,c)−0 = absolute conduction energy (~7-20 eV, inside the W2 bulk window). FEAST start vectors are dense over (n_cond_pad, n_val_pad, nk) with no pad mask, so on multi-device runs with px∤n_cond or py∤n_val the filter passes these channels and Rayleigh-Ritz reports spurious in-window eigenvalues with zero residual. px=py=1 and divisible band counts are immune.
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:558 'x = jax.random.normal(subkey, (1, n_cond_pad, n_val_pad, nk), dtype=x_dtype)' (no masking anywhere in file); bse_io.py:452-453 'eps_v, _ = _pad_axis_to_multiple(eps_v, axis=1, multiple=grid_y)' with :139-146 jnp.pad mode='constant' (zeros); matvec pad-decoupling: bse_ring_comm.py einsums contract X against psi (zero on pads)

claims.detail: Cache keys omit matvec and data identity, but the cached jitted closures capture both as constants. Second call in the same process with an equal key but different physics — run_feast_ritz(include_W=True) then include_W=False (RPA), or a different restart's data with equal shapes — silently reuses the first call's matvec AND arrays: RPA output is actually BSE output. Latent: current in-repo drivers (feast_sweep varies only n_quad/gamma/n_ritz on one dataset) do not trigger it; any cross-kernel or cross-dataset sweep in one process will.
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:128 'key = (max_iter, float(tol), str(dtype))' and :229 'key = (n_quad, n_ritz, max_iter, float(tol), float(ry_to_ev), str(dtype), bool(use_conjugate_symmetry))' — neither includes matvec/data; :132-139 _solve closes over matvec and data; :198 'solver = jax.jit(_solve)'

claims.symbol: TDA conjugate-symmetry accumulation + real Ritz projection
claims.detail: PLAUSIBLE / input-dependent. 'filt_i + two*jnp.real(w*y)' equals the true two-half-plane sum w·y + conj(w)·(z̄−H)⁻¹x only if conj(y) = (z̄−H)⁻¹x, i.e. conj((z̄−H̄)⁻¹x̄) with H̄=H (elementwise-real H) and x̄=x. _build_ritz_vectors then enforces 'float(c[j].real)' and 'v = v.real' (comment: BSE-TDA eigenvectors are real). Valid for real-gauge Si; for complex-Hermitian TDA H (SOC spinor psi, general k-gauges) the imaginary part of the filtered subspace is silently discarded — wrong eigenvectors/degraded eigenvalues. The default non-TDA path (explicit conjugate nodes, complex vectors) is unaffected.
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:251-252 'two = jnp.asarray(2.0, ...); filt_i = filt_i + two * jnp.real(w * y)'; :452-453 'v = sum(float(c[j].real) * filtered[j] ...); v = v.real  # BSE-TDA eigenvectors are real'; psi arrays are complex (bse_io loads complex128, spinor axis s in einsums kcsm)

claims.symbol: run_feast_ritz API defaults vs CLI defaults
claims.detail: Python API defaults quadrature='zolotarev', use_tda=True; CLI defaults --quadrature ellipse, full non-TDA. This drift already broke a caller: feast_sweep.py:173 calls run_feast_ritz without quadrature/lambda bounds, hitting the ValueError 'Zolotarev quadrature requires spectral bounds' (bse_feast.py:583-587) inside its per-config try/except — the entire sweep no-ops at HEAD (feast_sweep also reads rr_data.n_physical, a field RitzResult no longer defines — logged for the feast_sweep audit file).
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:476 'quadrature: str = "zolotarev"' and :477-478 'lambda_min_eV: float | None = None'; :1123-1125 CLI default='ellipse'; feast_sweep.py:173-186 passes neither quadrature nor bounds; RitzResult fields at bse_feast.py:294-303 lack n_physical, feast_sweep.py:210 'rr_data.n_physical'

claims.symbol: W_R construction via plain jnp.fft.ifftn on sharded W_q
claims.detail: W_R = jnp.fft.ifftn(W_q, axes=(2,3,4), norm='ortho') is computed up to three times per main() run (fp64 FEAST, fp32 GMRES shadow, fp32 bounds) using the plain FFT that bse_ring_comm explicitly works around with make_sharded_ifftn_3d because it forces a full all-gather of the sharded tensor; materializes the full mu_pad^2*nk W (e.g. 4000^2*64*16B ~ 16 GB) per device at production scale. run_feast_ritz also mutates the caller's data dict in-place (data['W_R'] = ...).
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:505 'data["W_R"] = jnp.fft.ifftn(data["W_q"], axes=(2, 3, 4), norm="ortho")'; bse_ring_comm.py:398-409 comment 'Plain jnp.fft.ifftn/fftn on a sharded tensor forces XLA to all-gather the entire array before the FFT' + make_sharded_ifftn_3d workaround; feast_sweep.py:613-614 caller confusion comment about who computes W_R

claims.detail: Bounds Lanczos always builds the TDA matvec even when use_tda=False; the full-spectrum lower edge is approximated as E_min = -|E_max| and diagonal transition energies are mirrored. Related: non-TDA Rayleigh-Ritz coerces eigenvalues real (evals = evals.real after np.linalg.eig) and the residual identity Re[c^H(G-2*lam*H+lam^2*S)c] is exact only for real lam — fine for positive-definite BSE, silently wrong if genuinely complex pairs appear. Approximations, printed as such — not bugs, but refactor targets when full-BSE matures.
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:734 'matvec = build_bse_ring_matvec(' (no _full variant, no use_tda branch); :825-826 'if not use_tda: e_min_ry = -abs(e_max_ry)'; :399-400 'evals = evals[order]; evals = evals.real'

claims.symbol: module import side effect + device residency
claims.detail: jax.config.update('jax_enable_x64', True) at import time flips global x64 for any importer. All heavy BSE tensors (psi, W_q, V_q0) are device-resident and sharded — the opposite of the gw/ convention of host-resident io_callback caches; --gmres-fp32 doubles residency with a complex64 shadow copy of all eight arrays kept alongside fp64 originals for Rayleigh-Ritz. Units/axes follow LORRAX-internal conventions (Ry, v=0 = deepest valence); the BGW valence flip + Ry->eV conversion happen only in bse_io.write_eigenvectors_stream per src/bse/STATUS.md, so no BGW-compat translation exists (or is needed) in this file.
claims.evidence: bse_feast.py:26 'jax.config.update("jax_enable_x64", True)'; :281-291 _build_gmres_data_fp32 second copies; :513-520 both dicts alive; STATUS.md 'Index ordering' items 1-2; MEMORY.md io_callback convention for gw

verdicts.reasoning: Verified bse_feast.py:1101 '--windows-kpm' is action='store_true' (default False), and the dispatch at 1199 'if args.windows_kpm: ... elif args.window1 is not None or args.window2 is not None:' (1224) makes window1/2 unreachable whenever windows_kpm is truthy. bse_jax.py:579 hard-appends the literal string '--windows-kpm' to the argv list passed to bse_feast.main() UNCONDITIONALLY (not inside any `*([...] if cond else [])` guard, unlike the --rpa/--tda/--gmres-fp32 flags on the same lines 576-578), while --feast-window1/2 are only conditionally appended afterward (592-601). Checked bse_jax.py's own argparse (330-410ish) for any bypass flag (--no-windows-kpm, --feast-windows-kpm-off, etc.) — none exists. So every `python -m bse.bse_jax --feast-ritz --feast-window1 ...` invocation always takes the KPM branch; --feast-window1/2 are dead weight from that call path. grep -rl 'bse_feast\|feast_sweep\|run_feast_ritz\|windows_kpm\|feast_window' --include='*test*.py' . -> no hits, so no test exercises or would catch this.

verdicts.symbol: run_feast_ritz random start vectors vs zero-padded bands
verdicts.reasoning: Verified bse_io.py:139-146 _pad_axis_to_multiple uses jnp.pad(..., mode='constant') (zero fill); called at 450-453 on psi_v_X (axis=1, multiple=grid_y=py), psi_c_X (axis=1, multiple=grid_x=px), eps_v (multiple=grid_y), eps_c (multiple=grid_x) -- i.e. padded valence index has BOTH psi_v=0 AND eps_v=0. Traced the matvec: bse_ring_comm.py's D_term = eps_c.T[...] - eps_v.T[...] broadcast directly onto X (lines 332-333, 433-435, 632-634) so a padded-valence diagonal element evaluates to eps_c(real)-0=eps_c(real), an unshifted physical conduction-band energy, not an excitation energy. The V/W encode/decode path (_encode_T, apply_V_ring, _apply_W_from_T) multiplies by psi_v_Y and psi_c_X at every contraction, so any component of X supported purely on a padded index contracts to zero going in AND the output row at that index is scaled by the same zero psi -- i.e. V,W both vanish on that index in both directions, making it an exact decoupled eigenchannel with eigenvalue = bare eps_c. Confirmed no masking exists anywhere in bse_feast.py (grep 'mask' -> zero hits); random start vectors at 558/563-564/667/672-673/747-748 are drawn dense over (n_cond_pad, n_val_pad, nk) with no exclusion of padded indices. This only manifests when px does not divide n_cond or py does not divide n_val (px=py=1 or divisible band counts are immune, matching the claim's own caveat), so it is a real but narrow/latent correctness gap, not a currently-triggered failure.

Verified bse_feast.py:128 'key = (max_iter, float(tol), str(dtype))' in _get_gmres_solver, and the inner `_solve(b, diag_h, z)` closure at 132-196 references `matvec` and `data` from the ENCLOSING function's parameters, not from the jit-traced args -- these become baked-in Python closures, not JAX tracer inputs. `solver = jax.jit(_solve)` is stored under `key` at line 199 with no matvec/data component. Same pattern confirmed for _FEAST_RUNNER_CACHE at 229 (key omits matvec/data; `_run` closure at 233-266 calls gmres_solve_sharded_jit which re-derives matvec/data internally via _get_gmres_solver, which itself is keyed the same stale way). A second call in-process with an equal (max_iter,tol,dtype) key but a genuinely different matvec or data (RPA vs BSE include_W, or a different restart dataset of identical shape) will short-circuit at the cache-hit branch and silently execute the FIRST call's captured matvec/data. Confirmed this is dormant given current callers: only feast_sweep.py calls run_feast_ritz repeatedly (varying n_quad/gamma/n_ritz/window on ONE fixed dataset with fixed include_W), so the closed-over matvec/data never actually differ across cache hits in any in-repo driver today -- but the design is a live landmine for any future cross-kernel or cross-dataset loop in one process, exactly as claimed.

verdicts.symbol: TDA conjugate-symmetry accumulation + real Ritz projection
verdicts.reasoning: Re-derived the math: for a two-pole conjugate sum w*y + w̄*(z̄-H)^{-1}x to equal 2*Re(w*y) (bse_feast.py:251-252 'filt_i + two*jnp.real(w*y)'), one needs (z̄I-H)^{-1}x = conj(y) = conj((zI-H)^{-1}x). This holds iff H is elementwise-real (not merely Hermitian) and x is real -- for Hermitian-but-complex H, (z̄I-H)^{-1} != conj((zI-H)^{-1}) in general (H^T=conj(H) for Hermitian H, so conj(zI-H)=z̄I-H^T, which only equals z̄I-H when H is symmetric, i.e. real elementwise). Confirmed x IS drawn real (jnp.float64/float32, bse_feast.py:556-558) satisfying half the precondition. Confirmed at 452-453 '_build_ritz_vectors' additionally coerces `v = v.real` with comment 'BSE-TDA eigenvectors are real'. Checked for any guard restricting this path to real-gauge/non-spinor systems: `grep -n 'nspinor|real.gauge|assert.*tda' bse_feast.py` -> zero hits. bse_io.py loads psi as complex128 generically with a spinor axis 's' used throughout (kcsm einsums), and STATUS.md documents spinor/SOC BSE as a supported configuration elsewhere in this module ('--n-reorth -1 ... is the right default for spinor BSE'). So for any complex-Hermitian TDA H (SOC bispinor psi, or a general complex k-gauge) this formula silently drops the true correction term and forcibly discards Im(subspace) -- a genuine, derivable correctness gap, correctly scoped by the reader as input-dependent rather than universally wrong (real-gauge Si is unaffected; the default non-TDA path uses explicit conjugate nodes and is unaffected per the claim, confirmed by code structure at bse_feast.py:595-597 concatenating z_nodes with np.conj(z_nodes) explicitly rather than relying on the 2*Re trick).

verdicts.symbol: run_feast_ritz API defaults vs CLI defaults
verdicts.reasoning: Reclassified from 'convention' to 'confirmed-bug': this is not a benign naming/default-drift note, it demonstrably breaks a real caller. Verified bse_feast.py:476-478 run_feast_ritz defaults quadrature='zolotarev', lambda_min_eV/lambda_max_eV=None vs CLI argparse default='ellipse' at 1123. feast_sweep.py:173-186 is the ONLY call site of run_feast_ritz in that file (grep confirmed) and passes neither `quadrature=` nor lambda bounds, so it falls through to the zolotarev default; bse_feast.py:582-587 'if quadrature=="zolotarev": if lambda_min_eV is None or lambda_max_eV is None: raise ValueError(...)' fires on EVERY sweep config. This is caught by feast_sweep.py's own per-config `except Exception as e: print(f'FAILED: {e}'); continue` (187-189), so `run_sweep` silently no-ops for every configuration -- confirmed by inspecting that this try/except wraps the only run_feast_ritz call and there is no fallback path. Independently confirmed the claimed follow-on defect: RitzResult dataclass fields at bse_feast.py:294-303 are (ritz_evals, ritz_coeffs, s_evals, rayleigh_quotients, rel_residuals, n_total, s_floor) -- no `n_physical` field -- while feast_sweep.py:208 reads `rr_data.n_physical`, which would AttributeError even if the ValueError were fixed. grep confirms feast_sweep.py/feast_zolo_sweep.py/feast_ellipse_mixed_sweep.py are not referenced by any runs/*.sh, skills/*.md, or test file -- it's a standalone `python -m bse.feast_sweep` analysis tool, currently non-functional at HEAD due to this API drift.

verdicts.symbol: W_R construction via plain jnp.fft.ifftn on sharded W_q
verdicts.reasoning: Confirmed three occurrences of the plain unsharded FFT on W_q: bse_feast.py:505 (fp64 FEAST path), :516 (fp32 GMRES shadow), :714 (spectral-bounds Lanczos) -- all `jnp.fft.ifftn(...W_q..., axes=(2,3,4), norm='ortho')` with no sharded-FFT wrapper. Confirmed bse_ring_comm.py:394-401 documents exactly this JAX all-gather-before-FFT behavior for tensors sharded on axes other than the FFT axes, and provides `make_sharded_ifftn_3d`/`make_sharded_fftn_3d` as the fix, already used for the analogous T_R/T_k tensor at 404-407 (same P('x','y',None,...) sharding pattern as W_q per bse_ring_comm.py:881/943 `with_sharding_constraint(W_q, sh.W)`). Output correctness is unaffected (this is a materialization/performance/side-effect issue, not wrong physics), so 'refactor' is the right kind. Also confirmed `data['W_R'] = ...` (505) mutates the caller-supplied dict in place, since `data` is passed by reference and dict item assignment is a real side effect visible to the caller after run_feast_ritz/estimate_spectral_bounds_sharded return.

Verified via grep -rn 'def _create_mesh_xy|def create_mesh_2d' src/bse/: exact 5 verbatim redefinitions at bse_pseudopoles.py:203, bse_feast.py:686, bse_w_exact.py:28, feast_zolo_sweep.py:98, feast_ellipse_mixed_sweep.py:74, plus a 6th distinct mesh-builder `create_mesh_2d` in bse_ring_comm.py:31. Confirmed bse_kpm.py:28 and feast_sweep.py:32 import `_create_mesh_xy` from bse_feast rather than redefining it (used at bse_kpm.py:361, feast_sweep.py:570), showing the import path already works and the other 4 copies are genuinely avoidable duplication. Matches the sandbox's 'no redundancy in refactors' rule directly; not a correctness bug, purely a consolidation target.

Verified feast_accuracy_notes.md:75-86 documents '--n-quad {N_QUAD} --gamma {GAMMA}' as the sweep template flags. Verified bse_feast.py's actual argparse (dumped all add_argument calls 1077-1152): the flags are '--n-quad1'/'--n-quad2' (1093,1095) and there is no '--gamma' argument anywhere -- gamma is hard-coded `ELLIPSE_GAMMA_FIXED = 0.2` (line 30) and applied at 1246 'gamma=ELLIPSE_GAMMA_FIXED' regardless of quadrature type, confirmed unconditional (not gated on args.quadrature=='ellipse'). This is stale documentation plus a meaningless field fill for zolotarev specs -- no runtime effect since QuadratureSpec.gamma isn't read by the zolotarev path, just visually confusing. Correctly scoped by the reader as cruft, not a functional bug.

Verified bse_feast.py:734 'matvec = build_bse_ring_matvec(...)' in estimate_spectral_bounds_sharded has no use_tda branch and no build_bse_ring_matvec_full alternative (unlike run_feast_ritz at 485-500, which does branch cleanly on use_tda) -- confirmed the TDA-only matvec is always used for bounds estimation even when use_tda=False. Confirmed :826 'if not use_tda: e_min_ry = -abs(e_max_ry)' is a stated mirror-approximation, printed as such. Confirmed :397-401 non-TDA Rayleigh-Ritz: `np.linalg.eig(A)` (general eig, not eigh) followed immediately by `evals = evals.real` (discarding any imaginary component), and the residual formula at :410 `(G_np - 2*lam*H_np + lam**2*S_np)` uses this already-real lam, which is the exact residual identity only for real lambda. These are self-consistent, documented approximations rather than silent errors (the reader flags this explicitly as 'not bugs, but refactor targets when full-BSE matures'), so I mark refactor-target rather than confirmed-bug -- accurate as a code-quality/completeness gap for the non-TDA path, not a demonstrated wrong-output scenario in a currently exercised configuration.

verdicts.symbol: module import side effect + device residency
verdicts.reasoning: Verified bse_feast.py:26 'jax.config.update("jax_enable_x64", True)' at module import time (global side effect on any importer, confirmed exact line). Verified device residency: bse_io.py's _read_psi_mu_sharded calls jax.device_put + jax.make_array_from_process_local_data (lines 213-215), i.e. psi/W_q/V_q0 are device-resident sharded arrays, not host io_callback caches -- a real deviation from the MEMORY.md gw/ io_callback convention, but BSE psi tensors are the array being solved over (needed every matvec), unlike GW's large read-only host caches pulled per-slice, so this is an architectural choice not obviously wrong for this module. Verified `--gmres-fp32` doubles residency: _build_gmres_data_fp32 (281-291) casts exactly 8 keys (psi_c_X, psi_c_Y, psi_v_X, psi_v_Y, eps_c, eps_v, V_q0, W_q) to fp32/complex64, matching the claim's 'all eight arrays'. Verified STATUS.md:29-34 confirms the BGW valence-flip and Ry->eV conversion happen only in bse_io.write_eigenvectors_stream, with no BGW-compat translation in bse_feast.py -- correctly self-scoped by the reader as 'no translation exists (or is needed) in this file', i.e. this claim accurately describes a convention rather than asserting a defect.
